title: Retirement Residence Permit and Tax Benefits - Portugal
title_local: Visto de Residencia para Aposentados / Residente Nao Habitual (RNH)
summary: British nationals who wish to retire in Portugal generally use the D7 passive income visa route and then obtain residence status in Portugal.
steps: Apply for the D7 Passive Income Visa at the Portuguese consulate in your country.
Wait for visa approval and any additional requests.
Travel to Portugal with the D7 visa and complete the residence process within validity window.
Book and attend the immigration appointment with AIMA for residence permit issuance.
Register for tax status options at the Portuguese Tax Authority, where eligible.
documents: Proof of passive income (pension, investment, or rental income)
Criminal record check from home country (apostilled where required)
office: Portuguese Consulate (visa stage) and AIMA in Portugal (residence stage).
fee: EUR90 visa fee; EUR83 residence permit fee
notes: Tax regimes and eligibility rules can change, confirm current Tax Authority guidance before planning.
